page: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-4GB is a budget-tier virtual server configured with a single shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This single-core, single-threaded instance is backed by the KVM hypervisor and includes 4.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 30 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. The AMD EPYC 9575F processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, ranking in the top 10%. This high single-core capability is also reflected in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks, both of which score in the top 10%. However, due to the single-core constraint, multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is generally limited, with read-only bandwidth benchmarks categorized as weak and the PassMark memory score ranking in the poor category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a low-cost entry point with high single-threaded compute capability, but it presents significant resource density tradeoffs. The shared CPU allocation and weak multi-core scaling make it unsuitable for parallel processing, heavy database operations, or high-throughput web serving, as reflected in its weak PassMark database and static web serving benchmarks. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic utilities, development environments, or simple background tasks that do not require sustained multi-core performance or high memory bandwidth.
description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-4GB is a budget-tier virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPU running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, a 30 GB local SSD, and one public IPv4 address under the KVM hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, but weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core tests and single-threaded gzip compression, but falls into the bottom 25% for multi-core tasks, static web serving, and database operations. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic development environments, and simple background utilities.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-4GB is a virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 30 GB SSD. It offers top-tier single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining top-tier single-core CPU performance with a shared-core architecture for lightweight workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 30 GB of local SSD storage
